evals.prompt: Limpa a KB — quero verificar se há duplicados ou contradições antes de fazermos o digest.
evals.expected_output: A KB enrichment verdict memo classifying all high-similarity pairs above 0.95 as exact-duplicate / contradiction / fine, with cited sources for every pair, ingested under 'enrichment/YYYY-MM-DD.md'.
expectations: The skill calls kb_enrich_report(threshold=0.95, max_pairs=25) first and records pairs, rows_scanned, scopes, and threshold from the response.
Every pair classification (exact-duplicate / contradiction / fine) is based on reading the full chunk text — not the 120-char preview alone.
Each contradiction record quotes both claims with their [source: <path>] citations and names the specific incompatibility.
The verdict memo is structured with the three sections: Exact duplicates, Contradictions, Fine — related but distinct.
The verdict memo is ingested via kb_ingest with source 'enrichment/YYYY-MM-DD.md' before any routing is done.

evals.prompt: Run the KB enrichment pass — check for near-duplicates and contradictions in the knowledge base.
evals.expected_output: An enrichment verdict memo with each pair classified, contradictions routed to specific dept heads with named resolution instructions, and the memo ingested.
expectations: If kb_enrich_report returns 0 pairs, the skill writes a brief no-pairs note and ingests it as 'enrichment/YYYY-MM-DD.md' — it does not skip the ingest step.
Each contradiction routing note names the specific dept head (e.g. CTO for a tech architecture conflict, CFO for a pricing conflict) — not 'someone should look at this'.
The skill never deletes any chunk automatically — the verdict recommends deletion for human review only.
For any pair with cosine ≈ 1.0 and same source, the first-run legacy duplicate protocol is triggered and the pair ids are documented for manual cleanup.
Contradictions are routed by delivering a specific routing note naming the dept head, the conflicting claims, and the two source paths.

evals.prompt: Verificar duplicados na KB — parece que temos muitos chunks repetidos sobre a estratégia de preços.
evals.expected_output: A targeted enrichment pass surfacing pricing-related near-duplicates, classifying them as duplicates or fine pairs, and routing any pricing contradictions to the CFO/CRO.
expectations: The skill calls kb_enrich_report and focuses its classification on chunks mentioning pricing strategy.
The 'fine — related but distinct' classification includes an explicit assessment of why the pricing chunks are intentionally distinct (e.g. different time periods, different product lines).
Any pricing contradiction is routed to the CFO and/or CRO by name with the conflicting claims quoted.
The cosine score is recorded for every pair in the verdict memo alongside the source paths.
The verdict memo is ingested with source 'enrichment/YYYY-MM-DD.md'.
